action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 8's debug script revealed that localization_sensor[3] does NOT match the gps_compass angle frame (diff_loc=1.95 rad vs gps_angle=-0.36 rad). This explains why the pathfinder branch in model.py was steering the robot completely wrong — it computed rel_yaw using localization_sensor[3] which is in a different coordinate system. The pathfinder made performance worse (nav_to_pos_success 0.30 vs 0.56 with pure GPS). Analysis: I can fix the pathfinder without knowing the global yaw by using a delta-angle trick. The angle to any point in the robot's local frame = gps_compass_angle_to_human + (global_angle_to_point - global_angle_to_human), because both local angles share the same robot-yaw offset. This eliminates the need for localization_sensor entirely. I will also make the local controller more aggressive (higher forward gain, minimum forward in safe zone) to keep up with the moving humanoid. Decision: Rewrite model.py with (1) corrected pathfinder using delta_global, (2) snap_path points to navmesh, (3) more aggressive local controller, (4) remove localization_sensor usage. Update test_hybrid.py to verify pathfinder triggers. Run local test, then submit eval.
